reports.text:
Community 0 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Pankaj Chauhan
  PhDStudent: Namrata Kotwal, Himani, Yaseen Hussain, Manisha Sharma, Deepak Sharma, Solaim
  ResearchArea: Spectroscopy, Teaching  Interests, Synergistic Catalysis, Photochemical & Elecrochemical Organic Synthesis, Photoredoxcatalysis, Carbene Chemistry, Asymmetric synthesis, Asymmetric synthesis, Homepage: [https://www.pankajchauhanresearch.com](https://www.pankajchauhanresearch.com/), Domino/Cascade Reactions, Synergistic Photo-Organocatalysis, Mechanochemical Synthesis, Organic Chemistry, Asymmetric Organocatalysis, Asymmetric synthesis and Electrocatalysis
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 13, SUPERVISED_BY: 6, STUDIES: 6

Summary: The Community 0 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Professor Pankaj Chauhan, who has a research focus on spectroscopy, synergistic catalysis, and photochemical/electrochemical organic synthesis. The group consists of six PhD students, including Namrata Kotwal, Himani, Yaseen Hussain, Manisha Sharma, Deepak Sharma, and Solaim, who are supervised by Professor Chauhan. The research areas of this community include carbene chemistry, asymmetric synthesis, and electrocatalysis, among others, as evident from their research interests and publications.

reports.text:
Community 1 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Nityananda Sahu
  PhDStudent: Simran Sharma
  ResearchArea: Explicit Hydration, Reaction Mechanism and Reactivity, Vibrational IR/Raman Spectroscopy, Large Molecules and Molecular Clusters, Electronic Structure Calculations, Electroweak Quantum Chemistry: Chirality and Parity Violation, Development of Fragmentation methods, Theoretical and Computational Chemistry, Quantum Tunnelling
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 9, SUPERVISED_BY: 1, STUDIES: 1

Summary: The Community 1 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Dr. Nityananda Sahu, with Simran Sharma as a PhD student under his supervision. This group focuses on theoretical and computational chemistry research areas, including explicit hydration, reaction mechanisms, vibrational spectroscopy, and quantum tunnelling. Their work also involves the development of fragmentation methods and electroweak quantum chemistry, specifically in chirality and parity violation.
reports.summary: The Community 1 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Dr. Nityananda Sahu, with Simran Sharma as a PhD student under his supervision. reports.text:
Community 2 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Anil Kumar Paidi
  ResearchArea: 1. **Solid State Chemistry** - Investigation of Novel Oxides Synthesis, 3. **Investigating Electrode Materials in Real-time -** Employing state of art techniques to study electrode materials in real-time (in-situ), and development of structure-property relationships in new functional inorganic materials, 2. **Energy Storage Materials -** Synthesis, characterization, and Electrochemical study Alkali metal-ion battery systems
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 6

Summary: The Community 2 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Anil Kumar Paidi. The group focuses on three primary research areas: Solid State Chemistry, investigating novel oxides synthesis; Electrode Materials, employing state-of-the-art techniques to study electrode materials in real-time; and Energy Storage Materials, synthesizing, characterizing, and electrochemically studying alkali metal-ion battery systems. This community is connected through six relationships (RESEARCHES_IN), indicating a collaborative research environment within the department.
reports.summary: The Community 2 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Anil Kumar Paidi. The group focuses on three primary research areas: Solid State Chemistry, investigating novel oxides synthesis; Electrode Materials, employing state-of-the-art techniques to study electrode materials in real-time; and Energy Storage Materials, synthesizing, characterizing, and electrochemically studying alkali metal-ion battery systems. This community is connected through six relationships (RESEARCHES_IN), indicating a collaborative research environment within the department.

Summary: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Dr. Jayaramulu Kolleboyina and includes PhD student Haneesh Saini. The group's research focuses on developing advanced materials for various applications, including energy harvesting, water purification, and environmental technologies, with a specific interest in hybrid two-dimensional porous materials and their structures. They are connected to the department through research and academic supervision relationships.

Summary: The Community 5 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Professor Shivnath Mazumder, who focuses on computational chemistry and its applications. His group includes PhD students Neetu Singh and Sheetal Ranaut, who are likely working under his supervision. The research areas of this community encompass drug discovery, renewable energy, metal-catalyzed reactions, artificial intelligence in chemistry, and computer-aided catalyst discovery and drug design.

Community 6 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Department: Department of Chemistry
  Faculty: Shirsendu Ghosh, Avinash Dhamija
  ResearchArea: Exploring supramolecular self-assembly for advanced material development, include, Development of Super/high resolution optical microscopy and Single Molecule Spectroscopy technique, Developing porous organic cages for functional applications, Developing organic photovoltaic materials for sustainable energy conversion and next-generation optoelectronic devices, Controlling molecular assembly processes using flow chemistry/microfluidic platforms for efficient and scalable material synthesis, Allosteric Signaling in Proteins, Membrane Biophysics, Immunological signaling pathways
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 9, MEMBER_OF: 2

Summary: The Community 6 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Shirsendu Ghosh and Avinash Dhamija. This group focuses on interdisciplinary research areas including supramolecular self-assembly, advanced materials development, and single molecule spectroscopy. Their work spans various fields such as material science, biophysics, and optoelectronics, with a focus on developing sustainable energy conversion technologies and efficient material synthesis methods.

reports.text:
Community 8 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Subhas Samanta
  PhDStudent: Shivali Hans, Swati Rani, Robindo Chatterjee
  ResearchArea: Transition Metal Complexes Containing Macrocyclic Ligands & their reactivity, Organometalic catalysis involving non-innocent ligands, Synthesis and Reactivity of Transition Metal Complexes Biomimetic Catalysis, Bio-inspired transition metal catalysis, Transition metal complexes for biomimetic oxidation reaction, Small molecule activation reaction
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 6, SUPERVISED_BY: 3, STUDIES: 3

Summary: The Community 8 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Dr. Subhas Samanta, a faculty member with expertise in transition metal complexes. The group consists of PhD students Shivali Hans, Swati Rani, and Robindo Chatterjee, who are supervised by Dr. Samanta. Their research focuses on various areas, including transition metal catalysis, biomimetic catalysis, and small molecule activation reactions, involving non-innocent ligands and macrocyclic complexes.
